title: 7 Reasons Your Prayer Feels Unanswered (Quick Audit)
hook: Your prayer is heard—here is what to check next.
script: Your prayer is heard—here is what to check next. First, examine your motives; James 4:3 warns wrong desires can block answers, so ask God to purify your heart with Psalm 139. Second, release unforgiveness—Mark 11:25 invites letting go so prayers flow. Third, remember God's timing; some answers are seasons, not seconds. Fourth, pray through spiritual warfare with Scripture and persistence. Fifth, check your persistence—Luke 18 calls us to keep knocking. Sixth, are you being tested in stewardship? Be faithful in small things. Seventh, God may be redirecting—trust Romans 8:28. Download the 7-day Prayer Audit to walk it out.

title: If You're Tired of Unanswered Prayers, Listen
hook: If you're tired of unanswered prayers, listen to this.
script: If you're tired of unanswered prayers, listen to this. Sometimes silence isn't absence—it's an invitation to look inward. Ask God to search your heart with Psalm 139 and confess mixed motives. Name any bitterness and pray a guided release: 'Lord help me forgive.' Review how you steward time, money, and words; Luke 16 shows small faithfulness matters. If you spot a pattern, respond with humble repair, regular prayer, and simple acts of obedience. God hears honest hearts. Try the 7-day Prayer Audit to start practical change and hope.

title: When God Feels Silent: What He Might Be Doing
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this.
script: If God feels silent right now, watch this. Silence can mean God is working behind the scenes, testing timing, or redirecting you to something better. Practice patience by celebrating small mercies each day. If you sense resistance, pray persistent intercession and use Scripture as armor—Daniel shows how prayer meets opposition. Keep knocking; Luke 18 reminds us God honors persistence. If an answer isn't yes, ask, 'What are you being prepared for?' Reframe the wait as shaping, not stopping. Your prayers are heard—keep walking in faith.
